title: Building Meridian — An Autonomous AI Executive Assistant
excerpt: Meridian is an intelligent, full-stack application designed to act as a personal, autonomous executive assistant.
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:i}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(t){const r={code:"code",h2:"h2",h3:"h3",li:"li",p:"p",strong:"strong",ul:"ul",...t.components};return i(e,{children:[n(r.p,{children:"Meridian is an intelligent, full-stack application designed to act as a personal, autonomous executive assistant. By integrating deeply with a user's communication and scheduling tools, Meridian goes beyond simple task management. It actively triages inbound emails, intelligently detects and schedules calendar events, and provides a conversational interface for users to query their daily workflows."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"This case study explores the product architecture, the integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) via the Vercel AI SDK, and the backend engineering required to handle async data processing seamlessly in a modern Next.js environment."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"1. The Problem Context"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'The modern knowledge worker spends an inordinate amount of time on "meta-work"—managing the work rather than doing it.'}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Inbox Overload:"})," Users receive hundreds of emails daily, but only a fraction require immediate action. Traditional filters (like Gmail rules) are rigid and lack semantic understanding."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Scheduling Friction:"})," Organizing a meeting requires context switching: reading an email, checking a calendar, proposing times, and waiting for a response."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Fragmented Context:"})," To prepare for the day, a user must check their inbox, their calendar, and their task manager separately to build a mental model of their priorities."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"2. The Meridian Solution"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Meridian was engineered to consolidate these workflows into a single, AI-driven command center."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Key Product Features"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"AI Inbox Triage & Smart Chips:"}),' Meridian ingests emails and uses LLMs to classify them (e.g., Urgent, Read Later, Action Required). It extracts actionable "Smart Chips" (like a direct link to approve a document or track a package) right into the UI.']}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Intelligent Event Detection:"}),' When an email suggests a meeting ("Let's chat next Tuesday at 2 PM"), Meridian's EventDetectionService cross-references the user's free/busy calendar data and surfaces a one-click "Create Event" UI.']}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Agentic Chat Interface:"}),' A conversational UI (supporting voice input) where users can ask, "What are my priorities today?" or "Draft a polite decline to John's email." The agent uses function calling to search emails, read calendars, or send replies.']}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Asynchronous Daily Digests:"})," A cron-triggered job that synthesizes the previous 24 hours of emails and upcoming meetings into a concise, actionable morning brief."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"3. Technical Architecture & Stack"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Meridian is built on a modern, React-centric, serverless-first architecture optimized for AI workloads."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Frontend (Client)"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Framework:"})," Next.js 15 (App Router) maximizing React Server Components (RSC) for performance."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Styling & UI:"})," Tailwind CSS combined with Shadcn UI (Radix primitives) and Framer Motion for fluid, native-feeling animations."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"AI Integration:"})," ai and @ai-sdk/react (Vercel AI SDK) for streaming LLM responses and rendering Generative UI components on the fly."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Real-time Updates:"})," Server-Sent Events (SSE) to push background job completions (like email syncs) to the client without aggressive polling."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Backend (Server)"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Runtime:"})," Node.js (via Vercel Serverless Functions/Edge)."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Database:"})," PostgreSQL managed via Prisma ORM for robust, type-safe data modeling of Users, Emails, CalendarEvents, and AgentDecisions."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Background Jobs:"})," Redis combined with BullMQ. This is crucial for handling high-latency tasks like initial email synchronization and bulk LLM processing."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Integrations:"})," Google Workspace APIs (Gmail, Google Calendar) via NextAuth/Auth.js for secure OAuth 2.0 token management."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"4. Engineering Challenges & Solutions"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Building an AI agent that operates autonomously on a user's data presents unique challenges in state management, prompt engineering, and background processing."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge A: The Latency of AI Operations"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Standard web requests expect a response in under 500ms. However, running a newly received email through an LLM to generate a summary, classify its urgency, and extract action items can take 2 to 5 seconds. If this were done synchronously on page load, the UX would be unacceptably slow."}),"
",i(r.p,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Redis, BullMQ, and Webhooks"}),"
Meridian decouples data ingestion from AI processing."]}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Ingestion:"})," When a new email arrives, a webhook is triggered. Meridian simply saves the raw email payload to PostgreSQL via Prisma."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Queueing:"})," An event is immediately pushed to a BullMQ queue backed by Redis. The HTTP response is instantly returned as 200 OK, freeing up the web server."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Background Processing:"})," A dedicated background worker picks up the job. It prompts the LLM to analyze the email. Once the LLM returns the classification and summary, the worker writes an AgentDecision record to the database."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Client Notification:"})," The frontend subscribes to an SSE endpoint (",n(r.code,{children:"/api/sse"}),'). When the background worker finishes, it pushes a localized event to the client, which elegantly animates the new "Smart Chips" and AI summary onto the user's screen using Framer Motion.']}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge B: Bridging Chat and UI (Generative UI)"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'Users don't just want text responses; they want actionable interfaces. If a user says, "Schedule a meeting with Sarah for tomorrow," a raw text reply ("I have scheduled it.") is inferior to a rich, interactive calendar widget that the user can confirm or modify.'}),"
",i(r.p,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Vercel AI SDK Function Calling and RSC"}),"
Meridian leverages the Vercel AI SDK's tool-calling capabilities combined with React Server Components."]}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["In ",n(r.code,{children:"app/api/chat/route.ts"}),", the system defines tools like ",n(r.code,{children:"getCalendarFreeBusy"}),", ",n(r.code,{children:"searchEmails"}),", and ",n(r.code,{children:"renderEventModal"}),"."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["When the user asks to schedule a meeting, the LLM determines it needs to use ",n(r.code,{children:"renderEventModal"}),"."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["Instead of returning text, the server streams down a serialized React Server Component (",n(r.code,{children:"<CreateEventModal prefilledData={...} />"}),")."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["The client's ",n(r.code,{children:"AgentChatUI.tsx"}),' receives this component stream and renders it directly in the chat feed. The user can interact with this UI natively, click "Confirm," and trigger the actual Google Calendar API mutation.']}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge C: Managing Context Windows and Costs"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'Feeding an entire inbox into an LLM context window is mathematically impossible and financially ruinous. To provide a "Daily Digest," the system must synthesize vast amounts of data efficiently.'}),"
",i(r.p,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Map-Reduce Summarization Pipelines"}),"
Meridian's Digest engine (",n(r.code,{children:"app/api/cron/digest/route.ts"})," and ",n(r.code,{children:"lib/digest.ts"}),") uses a map-reduce strategy."]}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Map Phase:"}),' As emails arrive throughout the day, the background workers generate strict, 1-2 sentence summaries and assign a "priority score" (1-10) to each email.']}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Filter Phase:"})," At 6:00 AM, the Digest Cron Job fetches only emails with a priority score > 5, ignoring newsletters and cold outreach."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Reduce Phase:"}),' The system takes these pre-computed, dense summaries and feeds them into a single, final LLM prompt alongside the day's calendar events. The LLM is instructed to output a structured JSON response containing "Key Themes," "Required Actions," and "Meeting Prep."']}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"This architecture drastically reduces token usage, speeds up digest generation, and ensures the user only sees highly relevant information."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"5. Security & Data Privacy"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Because Meridian handles deeply personal data (inboxes and schedules), strict security protocols are baked into the architecture:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"OAuth Least Privilege:"})," Integrations use incremental authorization. Meridian only requests read-only access to Gmail unless the user explicitly grants send permissions."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Token Security:"})," NextAuth securely encrypts and manages Refresh Tokens. Access tokens are never exposed to the client-side JavaScript."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Stateless AI Proxies:"})," Prompts sent to external AI providers do not contain PII unless strictly necessary for the query, and strict data retention agreements (zero-retention policies) are utilized via the API provider tiers."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"6. Conclusion"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Meridian showcases the next evolution of productivity software: moving from passive dashboards to active, agentic systems. By aggressively utilizing Next.js App Router capabilities, offloading intensive LLM tasks to BullMQ background workers, and bridging the gap between text and UI using Vercel's Generative UI, Meridian delivers a low-latency, highly intelligent assistant."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'It proves that with the right orchestration of modern web tools and LLM function calling, we can effectively abstract away the tedious "meta-work" of daily life, allowing users to focus on what actually matters.'})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:i}=e.components||{};return i?n(i,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};

title: Somnia: A High-Performance, Open-Source Typeform Alternative
excerpt: Building a powerful, open-source form builder and survey platform engineered to deliver a premium, interactive user experience.
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:i}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(t){const r={code:"code",h2:"h2",h3:"h3",li:"li",p:"p",strong:"strong",ul:"ul",...t.components};return i(e,{children:[n(r.p,{children:"Somnia is a powerful, open-source form builder and survey platform engineered to deliver a premium, interactive user experience comparable to industry leaders like Typeform. Built from the ground up as a robust monorepo, Somnia provides teams with an intuitive drag-and-drop form builder, advanced logic jumps, real-time multiplayer collaboration, and deep analytics."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"This case study explores the architectural decisions behind Somnia, specifically focusing on how the team utilized Turborepo, tRPC, Next.js, and an independent Express backend to deliver a highly interactive, end-to-end type-safe application capable of handling complex conditional routing and real-time state synchronization."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"1. The Problem Context"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Data collection is the lifeblood of modern businesses, but the landscape of form builders is heavily polarized."}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The UX vs. Cost Trade-off:"})," Standard tools (like Google Forms) are free but lack the visual polish and fluid user experience that drives high conversion rates. Premium tools (like Typeform) offer beautiful, conversational UX but come with exorbitant pricing tiers and aggressive vendor lock-in."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Collaboration Friction:"})," Most form builders treat form creation as a solitary task. When marketing, product, and design teams need to collaborate on a survey, they are forced to share credentials or constantly overwrite each other's work."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Complex Logic Execution:"})," Building a form that routes users down entirely different paths based on their previous answers (Logic Jumps) requires an intricate evaluation engine that must run flawlessly on the client side to avoid latency."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"2. The Somnia Solution"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Somnia was designed to democratize premium form building by offering an open-source alternative that doesn't compromise on enterprise-grade features or user experience."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Key Product Features"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Conversational Form Runner:"}),' A highly polished, distraction-free interface powered by smooth animations and full keyboard accessibility (press "Enter" to submit, "A/B/C" to select options), maximizing completion rates.']}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Advanced Logic Jumps:"})," A visual rule builder allowing creators to define intricate conditional routing based on multiple response variables."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Real-Time Multiplayer (Collaboration):"})," Google Docs-style collaboration where multiple users can edit a form simultaneously, seeing each other's cursors and changes in real-time."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Comprehensive Analytics:"})," A built-in dashboard tracking drop-off rates, average time-to-complete, and aggregated response data visually."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Enterprise Features:"})," Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), password-protected forms, scheduled expiration dates, and custom domains."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"3. Technical Architecture: The Monorepo Strategy"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"To maintain high developer velocity while separating concerns between the web frontend and a robust real-time backend, Somnia utilizes Turborepo to manage its architecture."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Workspace (apps/)"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"apps/web (Frontend):"})," Built with Next.js 15 (App Router) and React 19. It handles the marketing site, the dashboard, the form builder, and the highly optimized form runner. Styled with Tailwind CSS v4 and animated via Framer Motion."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"apps/api (Backend):"})," A standalone Node.js server running Express. Unlike traditional Next.js applications that rely solely on serverless API routes, Somnia requires a persistent, stateful backend to handle WebSockets (via Socket.io) for real-time collaboration."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Shared Packages (packages/)"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"The magic of Somnia's architecture lies in its shared workspaces:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"packages/database:"})," Houses the PostgreSQL schema and migrations using Drizzle ORM. Both the api and web apps can import standard DB models directly."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"packages/schemas:"})," Contains Zod schemas representing the core business logic (e.g., FormSchema, LogicJumpSchema). These are imported by the frontend for client-side form validation and by the backend for payload sanitization, ensuring zero discrepancy."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"packages/trpc:"})," The connective tissue. It defines the tRPC routers, context, and client wrappers, providing seamless, end-to-end type safety between the Express API and the Next.js frontend."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"packages/email:"})," Centralizes React Email templates (like CollaboratorInvite and ExpiryWarning) allowing them to be previewed and tested independently."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"4. Engineering Challenges & Solutions"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Building a platform that rivals a multi-million dollar SaaS product requires overcoming significant hurdles in state management, real-time communication, and UI rendering."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge A: Bridging Next.js and a Stateful Backend with Type Safety"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Because Somnia requires a persistent Express server for WebSockets (Socket.io), the standard Next.js Server Actions or API routes were insufficient for the entire backend load. However, splitting the frontend and backend usually introduces the classic REST API problem: keeping TypeScript interfaces synchronized across repositories."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Monorepo + tRPC"})}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Somnia leverages tRPC to completely eliminate the need for GraphQL or REST API documentation."}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["In ",n(r.code,{children:"apps/api/src/trpc/procedures.ts"}),", the backend defines strictly typed procedures (e.g., createForm, getAnalytics)."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["These procedures utilize the shared Zod schemas from ",n(r.code,{children:"packages/schemas"})," for input validation."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["The ",n(r.code,{children:"apps/web"})," frontend imports the generated tRPC React query hooks (useQuery, useMutation)."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The Result:"})," If a backend engineer renames a database field from title to formTitle in ",n(r.code,{children:"packages/database"})," and updates the tRPC router, the Next.js frontend compilation will instantly fail, highlighting the exact line of code that broke. This creates an unbreakable, type-safe contract across the network boundary."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge B: The Conversational Form Runner UX"}),"
",i(r.p,{children:["To compete with Typeform, the actual experience of filling out a form (",n(r.code,{children:"apps/web/components/FormRunner.tsx"}),") had to feel native, fluid, and completely frictionless. Native HTML forms are rigid and require page reloads or clunky scrolling."]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Framer Motion and State-Driven UI"})}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Somnia's Form Runner operates as a Single Page Application (SPA) state machine."}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"One Question at a Time:"})," The runner maintains a ",n(r.code,{children:"currentIndex"})," state. Only the active question is rendered in the DOM's primary view."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Fluid Transitions:"})," By utilizing Framer Motion's ",n(r.code,{children:"<AnimatePresence>"})," and custom ",n(r.code,{children:"PageTransition"})," components, Somnia calculates the slide direction (up for next, down for previous) and smoothly animates the old question out while bringing the new one into focus."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Ambient Polish:"})," The team built custom background components like ",n(r.code,{children:"FogCanvas"})," and ",n(r.code,{children:"IntroAnimation"})," to provide a deep, immersive visual aesthetic that standard SaaS tools lack."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Keyboard-First Navigation:"})," A custom ",n(r.code,{children:"useKeyboardNavigation"})," hook listens for global keystrokes. Users can press ",n(r.code,{children:"Cmd+Enter"})," to submit text, or press ",n(r.code,{children:"A"}),", ",n(r.code,{children:"B"}),", ",n(r.code,{children:"C"})," to select multiple-choice options, allowing power users to complete surveys rapidly without touching a mouse."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge C: Real-Time Multiplayer Form Building"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"When an organization is preparing a massive launch survey, multiple stakeholders need to edit the form simultaneously. Handling concurrent edits on a deeply nested JSON structure (a form with multiple blocks, options, and logic jumps) can quickly lead to data corruption or race conditions."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Socket.io and Optimistic Concurrency"})}),"
",i(r.p,{children:["While tRPC handles standard CRUD operations, Somnia's ",n(r.code,{children:"apps/api"})," server runs a persistent Socket.io instance."]}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["When a user opens the builder, they join a WebSocket room specific to that ",n(r.code,{children:"form_id"}),"."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["As a user types in a field (e.g., editing a question title), the client emits a granular ",n(r.code,{children:"field_update"})," event rather than sending the entire form payload."]}),"
",n(r.li,{children:"The server broadcasts this delta to all other clients in the room, which instantly update their local React state."}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Conflict Resolution:"})," For structural changes (like deleting a question), Somnia implements optimistic UI updates on the client side while relying on the backend's PostgreSQL database (via Drizzle ORM transactions) as the ultimate source of truth, ensuring that concurrent structural edits do not leave the form in an invalid state."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge D: Evaluating Complex Logic Jumps"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Forms are rarely linear. A user might set a rule: "If Question 1 is 'Yes' AND Question 3 is 'Enterprise', skip to Question 8." Evaluating this on the backend would cause unacceptable latency between every question."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Client-Side Graph Traversal"})}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Somnia compiles the form's logic jumps into a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) when the form data is fetched."}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["Inside ",n(r.code,{children:"apps/api/src/lib/compileFormSchema.ts"}),", the backend verifies that the creator hasn't accidentally created an infinite loop (e.g., Q2 points to Q1, which points to Q2)."]}),"
",n(r.li,{children:"The sanitized logic rules are sent to the frontend."}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["As the respondent fills out the form, the ",n(r.code,{children:"FormRunner"}),' maintains a local dictionary of responses. When the user clicks "Next", the client instantly evaluates the logic jump conditions against the responses dictionary in memory, calculating the exact ',n(r.code,{children:"nextIndex"})," in milliseconds without requiring a network request."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"5. Security & Access Control (RBAC)"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Because forms often collect Personally Identifiable Information (PII) or sensitive company data, Somnia implements a strict security posture:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Role-Based Access Control:"})," Built natively into the tRPC middleware, the system checks if a user is an OWNER, EDITOR, or VIEWER for a specific workspace before allowing mutations."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Token Security:"})," Authentication uses NextAuth.js. For public form respondents, a stateless JWT is generated when they begin a draft, ensuring their progress is saved securely in their browser without requiring an account, while preventing them from accessing other respondents' data."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Form Gating:"})," Creators can toggle password protection, which encrypts the payload on the server, only returning the questions to the client if the correct hash is provided. Forms can also be set to automatically close via a BullMQ background job based on a specified expiration date."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"6. Conclusion"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Somnia is a masterclass in modern full-stack TypeScript development. By leaning heavily into the Turborepo architecture, the team successfully maintained a clean separation of concerns—allowing a robust Express WebSockets server to handle real-time collaboration while providing the Next.js frontend with 100% type safety via tRPC."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Combined with a meticulous approach to UI/UX utilizing Framer Motion and an advanced client-side logic engine, Somnia stands as a highly performant, scalable, and beautifully designed open-source alternative in the data collection space."})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:i}=e.components||{};return i?n(i,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};

title: SwiftPolls: Real-time Audience Engagement
excerpt: Transforming audience engagement through real-time interactive sessions and asynchronous surveys with a robust TypeScript stack.
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:i}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(t){const r={code:"code",em:"em",h2:"h2",h3:"h3",li:"li",ol:"ol",p:"p",strong:"strong",ul:"ul",...t.components};return i(e,{children:[n(r.p,{children:"SwiftPolls is a modern, full-stack web application designed to transform audience engagement through real-time interactive sessions and asynchronous surveys. Built with a robust TypeScript stack, SwiftPolls bridges the gap between live presentation tools (like Kahoot or Mentimeter) and traditional form builders (like Google Forms), offering a unified platform for creators, educators, and teams to gather instant feedback and actionable insights."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"This case study explores the product design, technical architecture, and engineering solutions behind SwiftPolls, specifically focusing on how the platform handles high-concurrency real-time voting and seamless state synchronization."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"1. The Problem Context"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"In today's hybrid work and learning environments, keeping an audience engaged is notoriously difficult. Presenters often struggle with:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Tool Fragmentation:"})," Using one platform for live, interactive meeting polls and a completely different tool for post-meeting asynchronous feedback."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Friction in Participation:"})," Forcing users to create accounts or download apps just to answer a single question severely drops participation rates."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Performance Bottlenecks:"}),' "Thundering herd" scenarios where hundreds or thousands of participants submit votes at the exact same second can easily overwhelm a traditional relational database, causing latency or crashes.']}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"2. The SwiftPolls Solution"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"SwiftPolls was engineered to solve these exact pain points by offering two distinct modes of operation under one roof:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Live Mode:"})," Presenters generate a unique 6-character room code. The audience joins instantly via their mobile or desktop browsers without needing an account. The presenter controls the pace, pushing questions to audience screens and revealing real-time charts as votes stream in."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Async Mode:"})," Creators build a survey, set an optional expiration date, and share a public link. Respondents can answer at their own pace, and the system automatically closes the poll when the deadline hits."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Key Product Features"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Intuitive Poll Builder:"})," A drag-and-drop interface (powered by React Hook Form and Zod) to construct multi-question polls with custom settings (e.g., mandatory questions, live result visibility, time limits)."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Presenter Dashboard & Controls:"})," A dedicated interface for hosts to manage active sessions, toggle answer locks, push next questions, and end sessions."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Rich Analytics:"})," Beautiful, interactive dashboards utilizing Recharts to display bar charts, pie charts, and response trends."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Frictionless Audience Experience:"})," A mobile-optimized, real-time voting interface that updates instantly without page reloads."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"3. Technical Architecture & Stack"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"SwiftPolls employs a modern, type-safe stack across both the client and the server, ensuring developer velocity and application reliability."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Frontend (Client)"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Framework:"})," React 19 built with Vite for lightning-fast HMR and optimized builds."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Language:"})," TypeScript."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"State Management & Data Fetching:"})," TanStack Query (React Query) v5 handles server state, caching, and optimistic updates."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Real-time:"})," socket.io-client for persistent WebSocket connections."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Styling:"})," Tailwind CSS v4 for utility-first, responsive design, combined with lucide-react for iconography."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Forms & Validation:"})," react-hook-form paired with zod for strict client-side validation that mirrors backend schemas."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"The Backend (Server)"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Runtime:"})," Node.js with Express 5."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Database:"})," PostgreSQL, providing strong relational guarantees for users, polls, and response data."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"ORM:"})," Drizzle ORM, offering a lightweight, hyper-performant, and type-safe database layer."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"In-Memory Store:"})," Redis (via ioredis), utilized for caching, session management, and rate limiting."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Background Jobs:"})," BullMQ, backed by Redis, to handle intensive background tasks and scheduled events."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Real-time Engine:"})," Socket.IO server, deeply integrated with Express to broadcast state changes."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Authentication:"})," A dual-strategy approach using JWT (Access/Refresh tokens) and Passport.js for Google OAuth 2.0."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"4. Engineering Challenges & Solutions"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Building a platform that must instantly reflect state across multiple clients while safely recording data at scale presented several distinct architectural challenges."}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:'Challenge A: The "Thundering Herd" Voting Problem'}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"In a live session, a presenter might ask a question to an audience of 1,000 people. Human behavior dictates that a vast majority of those 1,000 people will tap their screens within a tight 3-to-5-second window."}),"
",i(r.p,{children:["If the backend executed a standard SQL ",n(r.code,{children:"UPDATE analytics SET count = count + 1"})," or ",n(r.code,{children:"INSERT"})," for every single incoming vote, the PostgreSQL database would face severe row-level lock contention and connection pool exhaustion, leading to massive latency spikes."]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Redis Buffering & BullMQ Flush Workers"})}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Instead of writing directly to Postgres, SwiftPolls utilizes Redis as a high-throughput, in-memory buffer."}),"
",i(r.ol,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Atomic Increments:"})," When a vote hits the ",n(r.code,{children:"/api/responses"})," endpoint, the backend uses Redis's highly optimized, atomic INCR command: ",n(r.code,{children:"redis.incr('analytics:${poll_id}:${option_id}')"}),". This operation takes microseconds and never blocks the database."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Real-time Broadcast:"})," The backend immediately broadcasts the updated counts to the specific Socket.IO room, ensuring the presenter's screen shows the bar charts climbing instantly."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"The Analytics Worker:"})," To ensure data permanence without hurting performance, SwiftPolls employs a BullMQ repeatable job named ",n(r.code,{children:"analytics-snapshot"}),". Every 10 seconds, this worker wakes up:","
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["It scans Redis for all ",n(r.code,{children:"analytics:*"})," keys using a pipeline."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["It retrieves the current buffered counts and atomically decrements them (using ",n(r.code,{children:"DECRBY"}),")."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["It takes this aggregated batch of votes and performs a bulk ",n(r.code,{children:"UPSERT"})," transaction into PostgreSQL."]}),"
"]}),"
"]}),"
"]}),"
",i(r.p,{children:[n(r.em,{children:"Result:"})," The PostgreSQL database only receives a handful of optimized queries every 10 seconds, regardless of whether there are 10 or 10,000 concurrent voters."]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge B: Synchronizing Live Session State"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"In live mode, the audience's screens must precisely mirror the presenter's intent. If the presenter moves to Question 2, all audience devices must instantly render Question 2. If the presenter locks voting, all audience buttons must instantly disable."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: Socket.IO Rooms and Event-Driven State"})}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'SwiftPolls leverages Socket.IO's "Rooms" feature to segment traffic.'}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:["When a session starts, a unique ",n(r.code,{children:"room_code"})," is generated."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["Audience members join, emitting a ",n(r.code,{children:"join_room"})," event. The backend places their socket connection into that specific room."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:['The Presenter acts as the authoritative source of truth. When they click "Next Question", the client fires an API call and emits a ',n(r.code,{children:"next_question"})," WebSocket event."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["The backend updates the session's ",n(r.code,{children:"current_question_index"})," in PostgreSQL (for persistence if someone reconnects) and immediately broadcasts ",n(r.code,{children:'io.to(room_code).emit("question_changed", payload)'}),"."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:["The React client listens for these events via a custom ",n(r.code,{children:"SocketContext"})," and updates local state, triggering instant UI re-renders for the audience."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h3,{children:"Challenge C: Precision Scheduling for Async Polls"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:'Async polls allow creators to set an expiration date (e.g., "Close this survey on Friday at 5:00 PM"). The system must accurately close the poll at that exact millisecond and optionally notify the creator, without running expensive, continuous database polling (like a cron checking the DB every minute).'}),"
",n(r.p,{children:n(r.strong,{children:"The Solution: BullMQ Delayed Jobs"})}),"
",i(r.p,{children:["When a creator publishes an async poll with an ",n(r.code,{children:"expires_at"})," timestamp, the backend calculates the exact millisecond delay: ",n(r.code,{children:"delay = expiresAt.getTime() - Date.now()"}),"."]}),"
",i(r.p,{children:["It then adds a job to the BullMQ ",n(r.code,{children:"poll-expiry"})," queue with that specific delay. Redis holds this job efficiently in memory. When the precise moment arrives, the BullMQ worker wakes up, processes the job, sets ",n(r.code,{children:"is_active: false"}),' in the database, pushes a WebSocket event to boot any active respondents, and uses Nodemailer to send a "Poll Expired" summary email to the creator.']}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"5. Security & Data Integrity"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Handling user data and preventing abuse is critical for a polling platform."}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Rate Limiting:"})," To prevent malicious users from spamming votes, the ",n(r.code,{children:"express-rate-limit"})," middleware restricts the ",n(r.code,{children:"/api/responses"})," endpoints to 5 submissions per minute per IP address."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Vote Deduplication:"}),"
",i(r.ul,{children:["
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.em,{children:"Authenticated Users:"})," The backend enforces a unique constraint based on ",n(r.code,{children:"poll_id"})," and ",n(r.code,{children:"user_id"}),"."]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.em,{children:"Anonymous Async Polls:"})," The backend issues a unique ",n(r.code,{children:"session_token"})," via ",n(r.code,{children:"crypto.randomUUID()"})," when a user loads the poll, storing it in Postgres alongside the vote to prevent double voting from the same browser session. Client-side localStorage deduplication provides an immediate UX block."]}),"
"]}),"
"]}),"
",i(r.li,{children:[n(r.strong,{children:"Authentication Security:"})," JWTs are used for stateless auth. The short-lived Access Token (15 mins) is kept in memory/local state, while the long-lived Refresh Token (7 days) is securely stored in an ",n(r.code,{children:"HttpOnly"}),", ",n(r.code,{children:"Secure"}),", ",n(r.code,{children:"SameSite=none"})," cookie, drastically reducing the risk of XSS attacks stealing credentials."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"6. Conclusion"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"SwiftPolls demonstrates how modern web technologies can be orchestrated to build a highly responsive, scalable, and engaging platform. By offloading high-frequency write operations to Redis, utilizing BullMQ for asynchronous processing, and maintaining tight client-server synchronization via Socket.IO, the architecture elegantly sidesteps common bottlenecks associated with real-time applications."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"The resulting product provides presenters with a powerful, zero-latency tool to engage their audiences, wrapped in a clean, user-friendly interface that requires zero friction for participants to join."})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:i}=e.components||{};return i?n(i,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};
